title: Notification retry marks delivery successful after provider timeout
bug_description: When a provider call times out during a retry attempt, the system records the notification as delivered instead of leaving it retryable or failed.
expected_behavior: A timeout should be recorded as a non-success outcome, with delivery state left retryable or marked failed according to the retry policy.
actual_behavior: The retry path reports success and downstream status shows delivered even though the provider never confirmed receipt.

preconditions: A local notification worker or mocked retry path is available
A timeout fixture or provider mock can force a timeout on retry
steps: Trigger a notification retry using the timeout fixture or mocked provider timeout
Inspect the final delivery status and retry metadata
reproduction.expected_behavior: The record remains pending retry or failed, and delivery success is not recorded.
reproduction.actual_behavior: The record is marked delivered despite a provider timeout.

logs: Retry logs show timeout raised, but final notification state transitions to delivered

manual_checks: Run the timeout retry fixture again and confirm status no longer becomes delivered
Confirm a real provider success path still records delivery correctly

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if successful deliveries stop being recorded correctly
rollback.notes: Keep the last stable retry-state behavior and a successful delivery fixture for comparison.

scope_notes: Keep the fix inside notification send/retry status handling. Do not redesign the whole retry scheduler or provider abstraction unless the root cause clearly requires it.

notes.root_cause_hint: Likely a timeout exception path falling through to a success-state update or a shared finally block setting the wrong status
notes.risk_hint: A broad fix could break successful delivery accounting or create duplicate retries
similar_bugs: retry metadata incremented but status stayed successful
